english: -nas grágs-pa čé-ba (-nas grags pa che ba) famous, celebrated, from afar Mil.; rgyáṅ-du lús-pa (rgyang du lus pa) lingering far behind Sch.; rgyaṅ mig mi mtóṅ-mkán (rgyang mig mi mthong mkhan) W. short-sighted; rgyaṅ mčoṅ btáṅ-gin (rgyang mchong btang gin) moving forward by long leaps; rgyáṅ-so dzúgs-pa (rgyang so 'dzugs pa) to look over Sch., (ought perhaps to be spelled rgyaṅ-zo (rgyang zo) one looking, spying into a distance); rgyaṅ-śèl (rgyang shel) spy-glass, rgyaṅ-bsríṅs-pa (rgyang bsrings pa) lengthened to a great distance Lex.; rgyaṅ-páṅ (rgyang pang), rgyaṅ-péṅ (rgyang peng) n. of a philosophical (atheistical Cs.) sect in ancient India, Tar. 22, 8: jìg-rten-rgyaṅ-páṅ ('jig rten rgyang pang). — 2. used relatively: rgyaṅ-ríṅ-po (rgyang ring po) far, rgyaṅ-ríṅ-por soṅ (rgyang ring por song) he went far away Mil.; rgyaṅ-túṅ-ba (rgyang thung ba) near; rgyaṅ-grágs (rgyang grags) the reach of hearing, ear-shot, (gen. the distance at which the sound of a trumpet may be heard, i.e. about 500 fathoms; however, as this number is much in favour with the Tibetans, such estimates are not to be depended upon). — mig-rgyáṅ (mig rgyang) Glr. distance of sight, i.e. the distance at which a man may be well distinguished from a woman, or a horse from an ass; — rgyáṅs (rgyangs) adv. far, rgyaṅs bkyéd-de (rgyangs bkyed de) Mil.nt. moving far away, e.g. in order to increase one's distance from an unpleasant neighbour at table).
n. of a village and monastery in Tibet, not far from the frontier of Bhotan, Köpp. II., 358.

I. 1. ornament, decoration, rgyán-gyis brgyán-pa (rgyan gyis brgyan pa) decked with ornaments Dzl.; rgyan-dógs-pa (rgyan 'dogs pa) to adorn one's self Mil.; ka-rgyán (ka rgyan) an ornament at the mouth, edge, or brim of a vessel, e.g. peacock's feathers about the mouth of a búm-pa (bum pa) (sacred bottle), flowers in a glass etc.; kà-rgyán-čan (kha rgyan can) decorated in the front-part, e.g. a coat trimmed with fur of different colours, an arrow gaily painted at its notched end; rgyán-rnams (rgyan rnams) Dzl. ornaments, rgyán-ča (rgyan cha) id.; rgyan-gós (rgyan gos) Glr. festival garment, beautiful vesture; dbu-rgyán (dbu rgyan) an ornament of the head, a diadem. — 2. in relation to spiritual things: séms-kyi rgyan (sems kyi rgyan) something good, a blessing, for the heart Mil.; rgyán-du čar (rgyan du char) it proves a blessing, a moral advantage or benefit Mil. II. 1. a stake or pledge at play, = skugs (skugs), *gyan tsúg-če* (*gyan tsug ce*) W. to bet, to wager, e.g. a rupee: (also rgyal (rgyal)? Schr.). — 2. lot, rgyan rgyáb-pa (rgyan rgyab pa) to cast lots, without religious ceremonies, whereas rtags-ril (rtags ril) and mo (mo) are connected with such.

resp. sku-rgyáb (sku rgyab), Ssk. पृष्ठ 1. the back of the body, the back part of any thing; rgyáb-kyis pyógs-pa (rgyab kyis phyogs pa), in later literature also rgyab-pyógs-pa (rgyab phyogs pa) Thgy. to turn one's back to a person or thing, c. la (la), also fig. Dzl.; rgyáb-kyis pyógs-par byéd-pa (rgyab kyis phyogs par byed pa) to put to flight; rgyab stón-pa (rgyab ston pa) to turn one's back, to turn round Glr.; rgyáb-tu skyúr-ba (rgyab tu skyur ba) to throw to the back, to leave behind, to give up, to quit, frq.; rgyáb-tu bór-ba (rgyab tu 'bor ba) id.; rgyab brtén-pa (rgyab brten pa) to lean one's back against or upon, to lean or rest on, to rely upon, confide in Mil.; rgyab byéd-pa (rgyab byed pa) to protect Sch.; rgyáb-tu (rgyab tu), rgyáb-na (rgyab na), rgyáb-la (rgyab la) behind, behind hand; after, back; rgyab-nas (rgyab nas) from behind; rgyab mdùn yñis-la (rgyab mdun gnyis la) Glr., *gyab daṅ dún-la* (*gyab dang dun la*) col. behind and before; tí-seï byaṅ-rgyáb-tu sleb tse (ti se'i byang rgyab tu sleb tse) as we came behind to the north-side of the Tise Mil.; rgyáb-kyi skyed-mos-tsal-du yśégs-so (rgyab kyi skyed mos tshal du gshegs so) let us go into the garden behind us Dzl.; rgyab-rdzí (rgyab rdzi) one standing behind working people, in order to watch and superintend them. — 2. as much as one is able to carry on his back, a load, drel-rgyáb ysum (drel rgyab gsum) three mules' loads Glr. Comp. bal-rgyáb (bal rgyab), or *rgyab-bál* (*rgyab bal*) W. a fleece of wool. — rgyab-k'ál (rgyab khal) 'a back's load', a burden carried on the back Sch., Schr. — rgyab-sṅás (rgyab sngas) a cushion or pad for the back C. — rgyab-rtén (rgyab rten) something to lean against, a safe retreat, prop, support Mil., rgyáb-rtén byéd-pa (rgyab rten byed pa) to be a support Mil. — rgyab-ríṅ (rgyab ring) serpent, snake Sch. — *gyab-lóg jhe'-pa* (*gyab log jhe' pa*) C. 1. to turn one's back 2. to rebel, revolt; *gyab-lóg dád-če* (*gyab log dad ce*) W. to sit backward, with the back in advance. — rgyab-lógs (rgyab logs) the back, back-part, reverse of a thing

pf. and fut. brgyab (brgyab), imp. rgyob (rgyob), to throw, to fling, aiming at a certain point, hence to hit, also to beat with a stick, = rdúṅ-ba (rdung ba); to strike, mó-la mčus brgyáb-nas bsad (mo la mchus brgyab nas bsad) he (the male bird)
